untranslatable_reason: Signal requires ranking stocks by volatility and selecting bottom decile within a universe (FTSE World Developed Index constituents). This is a cross-sectional portfolio construction rule that requires universe-relative ranking and decile selection logic, which cannot be expressed as a single-stock technical indicator condition. The backtest engine would need portfolio-level ranking and selection capabilities not captured by the allowed indicators (which are all single-bar, single-stock metrics). The entry condition attempted to approximate 'low volatility' using hvol_20 below its mean, but this does not replicate the decile-ranking logic required.
translation_notes: The signal describes a factor-based stock selection strategy (low-volatility anomaly) requiring: (1) universe definition (FTSE World Developed), (2) cross-sectional volatility ranking across all constituents, (3) decile selection (bottom 10%), and (4) periodic rebalancing. This is fundamentally a portfolio construction rule, not a time-series entry/exit signal. A proper implementation requires a multi-leg ranking system external to single-stock technical indicators. The source text does not provide explicit rebalancing frequency, and no regime filters are specified.
